   263→    def create(self, request: Request, *args, **kwargs) -> Response:
   264→        test_hash = request.data.get("hash", None)
   265→        if not test_hash:
   266→            return Response("Missing hash", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
   267→
   268→        # validation on test params
   269→        test_hash = test_hash.strip()
   270→        # clean up test has if its a pretty one
   271→        test_hash = test_hash.replace("-", "")
   272→        if not test_hash.startswith("V"):
   273→            test_hash = test_hash.lower()  # in case it was added with caps letters
   274→
   275→        # THIS IS A CLEANUP HACK TO REMOVE LATER!
   276→        if settings.CONSULT_UPDATES_EMAIL:
   277→            emails = settings.CONSULT_UPDATES_EMAIL.split(",")
   278→        else:
   279→            emails = None
   280→
   281→        if test_hash in DUPLICATE_TEST_HASHES_ALREADY_ACTIVATED:
   282→            test_hash = test_hash + "0"  # add a zero to the end to make it unique
   283→            send_admin_email(
   284→                "Test %s has been activated. Was able to assign correct test type to LDT since wellness test was already activated, but needs manual review to confirm"
   285→                % test_hash,
   286→                "This test was assigned the LDT version test. Please review and confirm that this is correct and make sure the other test is marked as wellness. If not, please assign the correct test type to the user.",
   287→                to_addresses=emails,
   288→            )
   289→        elif test_hash in DUPLICATE_TEST_HASHES_NOT_ACTIVATED:
   290→            # first check if test is already activated, if so then just assign user to other one because that means we are good to go
   291→            test = Test.objects.filter(hash=test_hash).first()
   292→            zero_test = Test.objects.filter(hash=test_hash + "0").first()
   293→            if test.user_id and not zero_test.user_id:
   294→                test_hash = zero_test.hash
   295→                send_admin_email(
   296→                    "Test %s has been activated. Was able to assign correct test type, but needs manual review to confirm"
   297→                    % test_hash,
   298→                    "This test was assigned the LDT version test. Please review and confirm that this is correct and make sure the other test is marked as wellness. If not, please assign the correct test type to the user.",
   299→                    to_addresses=emails,
   300→                )
   301→            elif not test.user_id and zero_test.user_id:
   302→                test_hash = test.hash
   303→                send_admin_email(
   304→                    "Test %s has been activated. Was able to assign correct test type, but needs manual review to confirm"
   305→                    % test_hash,
   306→                    "This test was assigned the wellness version test. Please review and confirm that this is correct and make sure the other test is marked as LDT. If not, please assign the correct test type to the user.",
   307→                    to_addresses=emails,
   308→                )
   309→            elif not test.user_id and not zero_test.user_id:
   310→                # email doesn't match any or our sources darn... treat both as LDT and will need to handle manually
   311→                test_hash = test.hash
   312→                send_admin_email(
   313→                    "Test %s has been activated. Needs manual review to determine if it's wellness test or LDT"
   314→                    % test_hash,
   315→                    "Check if this test should be an LDT test or a wellness test. If wellness, then change test type to Wellness. If LDT, then trigger submitting lab order AFTER user submits health history. Make sure the other remaining test is changed to the appropriate type",
   316→                    to_addresses=emails,
   317→                )
   318→            elif test.user_id and zero_test.user_id:
   319→                # what the heck a 3rd person is trying to activate??
   320→                send_admin_email(
   321→                    "A 3rd user is trying to activate one of the special duplicate test hashes %s"
   322→                    % test_hash,
   323→                    f"User attempting to activate test is {request.user.email}",
   324→                    to_addresses=emails,
   325→                )
   326→                return Response("Test already claimed", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
   327→
   328→        # Check both Test and UrineTest models
   329→        test = get_test_by_hash(test_hash)
   330→        if not test:
   331→            return Response("Please enter a valid Test ID", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
   332→        if test.user:
   333→            return Response("Test already claimed", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
   334→
   335→        # Check if test is expired (both Test and UrineTest use same status constants)
   336→        if check_test_expired(test):
   337→            return Response(
   338→                "Your test has expired. Please write in to support@evvy.com for appropriate next steps",
   339→                status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
   340→            )
   341→
   342→        if check_test_deactivated(test):
   343→            return Response(
   344→                "Your test has been deactivated. Please write in to support@evvy.com for appropriate next steps",
   345→                status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
   346→            )
   347→
   348→        test = attach_user_to_test(request.user, test)
   349→        urine_test_enabled = get_urine_test_enabled(request.user)
   350→        is_urine = test.is_urine_test
   351→
   352→        ecomm_order = test.ecomm_order
   353→        if ecomm_order and not ecomm_order.user:
   354→            ecomm_order.user = test.user
   355→            ecomm_order.save()
   356→            if ecomm_order.order_type == Order.ORDER_TYPE_UNGATED_RX:
   357→                add_order_treatments_to_latest_existing_consult_if_exists_or_create_consult.delay(
   358→                    test.user.id, ecomm_order.order_number
   359→                )
   360→
   361→        if not is_urine:
   362→            properties: BrazeEventProperties = {
   363→                "testHash": test.hash,
   364→                "isLdt": test.is_ldt,
   365→                "isExpan": test.has_vpcr_test,
   366→                "hasProviderTestOrder": test.is_provider_ordered_sku(),
   367→                "providerTestPayer": test.get_provider_payer(),
   368→            }
   369→            track_event(
   370→                user=test.user,
   371→                alias_name=test.user.email if test.user else None,
   372→                event_name=BrazeEventName.TEST_KIT_ACTIVATED,
   373→                properties=properties,
   374→            )
   375→
   376→            if test.is_provider_ordered_sku():
   377→                associate_provider_test_order_to_test_by_email(
   378→                    test, request.user.email, {"from": "test_activation"}
   379→                )
   380→
   381→        # Handle UTI test-specific logic
   382→        if is_urine:
   383→            track_event(
   384→                user=test.user,
   385→                alias_name=test.user.email if test.user else None,
   386→                event_name=BrazeEventName.UTI_TEST_KIT_ACTIVATED,
   387→                properties={
   388→                    "testHash": test.hash,
   389→                },
   390→            )
   391→
   392→        serializer = (
   393→            serializers.UserUrineTestSerializer(test)
   394→            if is_urine
   395→            else serializers.UserVaginalTestSerializer(test)
   396→        )
   397→
   398→        data = serializer.data
   399→
   400→        # Add activation flow data if feature flag is enabled
   401→        if urine_test_enabled:
   402→            other_tests = get_other_unactivated_tests_from_order(test.hash)
   403→            test_type = "urine" if is_urine else "vaginal"
   404→            activation_flow: ActivationFlowData = {
   405→                "test_type": test_type,
   406→                "other_tests_available": len(other_tests) > 0,
   407→                "other_tests": other_tests,
   408→            }
   409→            data["activation_flow"] = activation_flow
   410→
   411→        return Response(data, status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)

   541→
   542→            properties: BrazeEventProperties = {

   566→    @action(methods=["post"], detail=True)
   567→    def consent(self, request, hash=None):
   568→        """
   569→        API to record consent to Evvy's privacy, terms, and conditions and create the lab order object.
   570→        Also handles multi-test activation flow and health history creation.
   571→
   572→        For the new flow (when urine_test_enabled feature flag is active):
   573→        - Creates lab order for the test (supports both Test and UrineTest)
   574→        - Returns other available tests from the same order
   575→        - Creates or update health history type, depending on tests activated/consented
   576→
   577→        Supports both Test and UrineTest models.
   578→
   579→        Query parameters:
   580→        - finalize: If True, create health history even if other tests are available
   581→                   (used when user has chosen not to activate other tests)
   582→        """
   583→        # Use get_object() which now handles both Test and UrineTest models
   584→        test = self.get_object()
   585→
   586→        # Create lab order/consent for this test
   587→        # LabOrder supports both Test and UrineTest via separate fields
   588→        create_lab_order_with_consent_for_test(test, get_current_time())
   589→
   590→        data = self.get_serializer(test).data
   591→
   592→        if get_urine_test_enabled(request.user):
   593→            other_tests = get_other_unactivated_tests_from_order(test.hash)
   594→            # Check if user wants to finalize (has chosen not to activate other tests)
   595→            finalize = request.query_params.get("finalize", "false").lower() == "true"
   596→            should_create_health_history, health_history_url, health_history_type = (
   597→                self._should_create_and_create_health_history(
   598→                    test=test, other_tests=other_tests, finalize=finalize
   599→                )
   600→            )
   601→
   602→            data["consent_flow"] = {
   603→                "other_tests_available": len(other_tests) > 0,
   604→                "other_tests": other_tests,
   605→                "should_create_health_history": should_create_health_history,
   606→                "health_history_url": health_history_url,
   607→                "health_history_type": health_history_type,
   608→            }
   609→
   610→        return Response(data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
   611→
   612→    @action(methods=["post"], detail=True, url_path="take-sample-later")
   613→    def take_sample_later(self, request, hash=None):
   614→        """
   615→        API to track when user chooses to take sample later from the activation consent modal.
   616→
   617→        This endpoint tracks the appropriate Braze event:
   618→        - For urine tests: UTI_TAKE_SAMPLE_LATER
   619→        - For vaginal tests: TAKE_SAMPLE_LATER
   620→
   621→        No data changes are made - this is purely for analytics tracking.
   622→        """
   623→        test = self.get_object()
   624→
   625→        if not test.user:
   626→            return Response(
   627→                {"error": "Test must be associated with a user"}, status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST
   628→            )
   629→
   630→        # Track appropriate event based on test type
   631→        if test.is_urine_test:
   632→            track_event(
   633→                user=test.user,
   634→                alias_name=test.user.email,
   635→                event_name=BrazeEventName.UTI_TAKE_SAMPLE_LATER,
   636→                properties={
   637→                    "testHash": test.hash,
   638→                },
   639→            )
   640→        else:
   641→            # Track generic event for vaginal tests
   642→            track_event(
   643→                user=test.user,
   644→                alias_name=test.user.email,
   645→                event_name=BrazeEventName.TAKE_SAMPLE_LATER,
   646→                properties={
   647→                    "testHash": test.hash,
   648→                },
   649→            )
   650→
   651→        # Return success response
   652→        serializer = self.get_serializer(test)
   653→        return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
   654→
   655→    def _should_create_and_create_health_history(
   656→        self, test: Test | UrineTest, other_tests: List[Dict], finalize: bool = False
   657→    ) -> Tuple[bool, str | None, str | None]:
   658→        """
   659→        Determine if health history should be created and create it if needed.
   660→
   661→        Health history creation logic:
   662→        1. If no other tests available → create health history now (single type)
   663→        2. If other tests available but user is consenting to second test → create/update to combined
   664→        3. If finalize=True → create health history even if other tests are available
   665→           (user has chosen not to activate other tests)
   666→
   667→        Args:
   668→            test: The test being consented to
   669→            other_tests: List of other unactivated tests from the same order
   670→            finalize: If True, create health history even if other tests are available
   671→
   672→        Returns: (should_create, health_history_url, health_history_type)
   673→        """
   674→        activated_vaginal_tests, activated_urine_tests = self._get_activated_tests_from_order(test)
   675→        has_vaginal = len(activated_vaginal_tests) > 0
   676→        has_urine = len(activated_urine_tests) > 0
   677→        has_other_tests = len(other_tests) > 0
   678→
   679→        # Case 1: Both test types activated → create/update combined health history
   680→        if has_vaginal and has_urine:
   681→            vaginal_test = activated_vaginal_tests[0]
   682→            urine_test = activated_urine_tests[0]
   683→            self._create_or_update_combined_health_history(vaginal_test, urine_test)
   684→            return True, f"/tests/{vaginal_test.hash}/context/", "combined"
   685→
   686→        # Case 2: No other tests available → create health history now
   687→        if not has_other_tests:
   688→            if has_vaginal:
   689→                vaginal_test = activated_vaginal_tests[0]
   690→                self._create_vaginal_only_health_history(vaginal_test)
   691→                return True, f"/tests/{vaginal_test.hash}/context/", "vaginal"
   692→            elif has_urine:
   693→                urine_test = activated_urine_tests[0]
   694→                self._create_urine_only_health_history(urine_test)
   695→                return True, f"/tests/{urine_test.hash}/context/", "urine"
   696→
   697→        # Case 3: Other tests available and user finalized → create single-type health history
   698→        if has_other_tests and finalize:
   699→            if has_vaginal:
   700→                vaginal_test = activated_vaginal_tests[0]
   701→                self._create_vaginal_only_health_history(vaginal_test)
   702→                return True, f"/tests/{vaginal_test.hash}/context/", "vaginal"
   703→            elif has_urine:
   704→                urine_test = activated_urine_tests[0]
   705→                self._create_urine_only_health_history(urine_test)
   706→                return True, f"/tests/{urine_test.hash}/context/", "urine"
   707→
   708→        # Case 4: Other tests available, first consent, not finalized → wait
   709→        return False, None, None
   710→
   711→    def _get_activated_tests_from_order(
   712→        self, test: Test | UrineTest
   713→    ) -> Tuple[List[Test], List[UrineTest]]:
   714→        """Get all activated tests from the same order as the given test."""
   715→        if test.ecomm_order:
   716→            order = test.ecomm_order
   717→            activated_vaginal_tests = list(Test.objects.filter(ecomm_order=order, user=test.user))
   718→            activated_urine_tests = list(
   719→                UrineTest.objects.filter(ecomm_order=order, user=test.user)
   720→            )
   721→            return activated_vaginal_tests, activated_urine_tests
   722→        elif test.is_vaginal_test:
   723→            return [test], []
   724→        else:
   725→            return [], [test]
   726→
   727→    def _create_vaginal_only_health_history(self, vaginal_test: Test) -> None:
   728→        """Create health history for vaginal test only."""
   729→        HealthContext.objects.get_or_create(
   730→            test=vaginal_test,
   731→            questionnaire_version=HealthContext.VERSION_3,
   732→        )
   733→
   734→    def _create_urine_only_health_history(self, urine_test: UrineTest) -> None:
   735→        """Create health history for urine test only."""
   736→        HealthContext.objects.get_or_create(
   737→            urine_test=urine_test,
   738→            questionnaire_version=HealthContext.VERSION_3,
   739→        )
   740→
   741→    def _create_or_update_combined_health_history(
   742→        self, vaginal_test: Test, urine_test: UrineTest
   743→    ) -> None:
   744→        """Create or update combined health history for both vaginal and urine tests."""
   745→        # Check if health history exists for vaginal test
   746→        try:
   747→            health_context = vaginal_test.healthcontext
   748→            # Don't combine if health history has already been submitted
   749→            if health_context.health_history_submitted_at:
   750→                # Health history already submitted, create separate one for urine test
   751→                HealthContext.objects.get_or_create(
   752→                    urine_test=urine_test,
   753→                    questionnaire_version=HealthContext.VERSION_3,
   754→                )
   755→                return
   756→            if not health_context.urine_test:
   757→                health_context.urine_test = urine_test
   758→                health_context.save()
   759→            return
   760→        except HealthContext.DoesNotExist:
   761→            pass
   762→
   763→        # Check if health history exists for urine test
   764→        try:
   765→            existing_health_context = urine_test.healthcontext
   766→            # Don't combine if health history has already been submitted
   767→            if existing_health_context.health_history_submitted_at:
   768→                # Health history already submitted, create separate one for vaginal test
   769→                HealthContext.objects.get_or_create(
   770→                    test=vaginal_test,
   771→                    questionnaire_version=HealthContext.VERSION_3,
   772→                )
   773→                return
   774→            existing_health_context.test = vaginal_test
   775→            existing_health_context.urine_test = urine_test
   776→            existing_health_context.save()
   777→            return
   778→        except HealthContext.DoesNotExist:
   779→            pass
   780→
   781→        # Check if health history exists for either test (edge case)
   782→        existing_health_context = HealthContext.objects.filter(
   783→            Q(test=vaginal_test) | Q(urine_test=urine_test)
   784→        ).first()
   785→        if existing_health_context:
   786→            # Don't combine if health history has already been submitted
   787→            if existing_health_context.health_history_submitted_at:
   788→                # Health history already submitted, create new combined one
   789→                HealthContext.objects.create(
   790→                    test=vaginal_test,
   791→                    urine_test=urine_test,
   792→                    questionnaire_version=HealthContext.VERSION_3,
   793→                )
   794→                return
   795→            existing_health_context.test = vaginal_test
   796→            existing_health_context.urine_test = urine_test
   797→            existing_health_context.save()
   798→            return
   799→
   800→        # Create new combined health history
   801→        HealthContext.objects.create(
   802→            test=vaginal_test,
   803→            urine_test=urine_test,
   804→            questionnaire_version=HealthContext.VERSION_3,
   805→        )

   807→    @action(methods=["post"], detail=True, url_path="ungated-checkout")

   821→
   822→        variant_ids = []

   828→        client = ShopifyAPIClient()

   909→    @action(methods=["post"], detail=True)
   910→    def submit(self, request, hash=None):
   911→        """
   912→        User submits the final health history form
   913→        """
   914→        test = self.get_object()  # returns Test object based on hash
   915→        context = test.healthcontext
   916→
   917→        # validation
   918→
   919→        if test.is_urine_test:
   920→            if not context.urine_sample_taken_at:
   921→                return Response(
   922→                    "Haven't taken urine sample yet", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST
   923→                )
   924→        elif not context.sample_taken_at:
   925→            return Response("Haven't taken sample yet", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
   926→
   927→        if test.status == Test.STATUS_ACTIVATED:
   928→            # test can be in TAKEN, or PROCESSING/READY (if they are filling form out after lab processing)
   929→            return Response(
   930→                "Test in invalid status. Take sample.",
   931→                status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
   932→            )
   933→        if context.health_history_submitted_at:
   934→            return Response("Already submitted", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
   935→
   936→        # TODO: could verify all of the 60 required fields on the health history form later
   937→
   938→        # make updates
   939→        context.health_history_submitted_at = convert_timezone(datetime.datetime.now())
   940→        context.save()
   941→
   942→        submit_lab_order_for_test(test, get_current_time())
   943→
   944→        # trigger analytics event
   945→        send_health_history_completed_klaviyo_event.delay(test.hash)
   946→        properties: BrazeEventProperties = {
   947→            "testHash": test.hash,
   948→            "isLdt": test.is_ldt if not test.is_urine_test else False,
   949→            "isExpan": not test.is_urine_test and test.has_vpcr_test,
   950→            "hhPrimaryReason": test.hh_primary_reason if not test.is_urine_test else None,
   951→            "hhType": get_health_history_type(test),
   952→        }
   953→        track_event(
   954→            user=test.user,
   955→            alias_name=test.user.email if test.user else None,
   956→            event_name=(
   957→                BrazeEventName.HEALTH_HISTORY_COMPLETED
   958→                if not test.is_urine_test
   959→                else BrazeEventName.UTI_HEALTH_HISTORY_COMPLETED
   960→            ),
   961→            properties=properties,
   962→        )
   963→
   964→        # serialize the final result using the correct serializer based on test type
   965→        serializer_class = self.get_serializer_class()
   966→        return Response(serializer_class(test).data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)

  1055→    # TODO: this will be deprecated once we finish rolling out shop all
  1056→    @action(methods=["get"], detail=True, url_path="treatment-recommendations")
  1057→    def treatment_recommendations(self, request, hash=None):
  1058→        test = self.get_object()
  1059→
  1060→        # Cache user prescriptions to avoid N+1 queries in serializers
  1061→        if test.user:
  1062→            from care.models import Prescription
  1063→
  1064→            self._user_prescriptions_cache = list(
  1065→                Prescription.objects.filter(user=test.user, deleted=False).select_related("product")
  1066→            )
  1067→        care_eligible = get_test_eligible_for_care(test)
  1068→        a_la_care_eligible = get_test_eligible_for_a_la_care(test, return_reason=False)
  1069→        if not care_eligible and not a_la_care_eligible:
  1070→            return Response({}, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
  1071→
  1072→        # Get or create the recommended treatment program once to avoid repeated expensive computations
  1073→        recommended_treatment_program = None
  1074→        if hasattr(test, "testresult") and care_eligible:
  1075→            # Use select_related to reduce database queries
  1076→            test.testresult.refresh_from_db()
  1077→            recommended_treatment_program = get_or_create_recommended_treatment_program(
  1078→                test.testresult
  1079→            )
  1080→            # Prefetch the treatments to avoid N+1 queries
  1081→            recommended_treatments = list(
  1082→                recommended_treatment_program.recommended_treatments.select_related(
  1083→                    "ecomm_product", "bundle_variant"
  1084→                )
  1085→            )
  1086→        else:
  1087→            recommended_treatments = []
  1088→
  1089→        # Filter out antibiotics/antifungals if user has previously purchased them
  1090→        filtered_recommended_treatments = (
  1091→            filter_antibiotic_antifungal_treatments_if_previously_purchased(
  1092→                recommended_treatments, test.user, test
  1093→            )
  1094→        )
  1095→
  1096→        # Pass the pre-computed recommended treatments to avoid recomputation
  1097→        available_a_la_care_treatments_for_test = get_all_available_treatments_for_test(
  1098→            test, filtered_recommended_treatments
  1099→        )
  1100→
  1101→        # Filter a_la_care treatments as well
  1102→        filtered_available_a_la_care_treatments = (
  1103→            filter_antibiotic_antifungal_treatments_if_previously_purchased(
  1104→                available_a_la_care_treatments_for_test, test.user, test
  1105→            )
  1106→        )
  1107→
  1108→        recommended_alc_treatments = get_recommended_treatments_for_a_la_care_for_test(
  1109→            test, recommended_treatment_program
  1110→        )
  1111→
  1112→        # Filter recommended a_la_care treatments
  1113→        filtered_recommended_alc_treatments = (
  1114→            filter_antibiotic_antifungal_treatments_if_previously_purchased(
  1115→                recommended_alc_treatments, test.user, test
  1116→            )
  1117→        )
  1118→
  1119→        # Add male partner treatment voucher if recommended and feature flag is enabled
  1120→        male_partner_voucher_product = None
  1121→        if should_recommend_male_partner_treatment(test):
  1122→            male_partner_voucher_product = EcommProduct.objects.filter(sku=MPT_VOUCHER_SKU).first()
  1123→
  1124→        # Create context with view for prescription caching
  1125→        serializer_context = {"view": self, "test": test}
  1126→
  1127→        data = {
  1128→            "transparent_bundle": serializers.UserTransparentBundleSerializer(
  1129→                {"recommended_treatments": filtered_recommended_treatments, "test": test},
  1130→                context=serializer_context,
  1131→            ).data,
  1132→            "a_la_care_treatments": serializers.UserGroupedALaCareTreatmentsSerializer(
  1133→                {"treatments": filtered_available_a_la_care_treatments, "test": test},
  1134→                context=serializer_context,
  1135→            ).data,
  1136→            "recommended_a_la_care_treatments": serializers.UserGroupedALaCareTreatmentsSerializer(
  1137→                {"treatments": filtered_recommended_alc_treatments, "test": test},
  1138→                context=serializer_context,
  1139→            ).data,
  1140→            "male_partner_voucher_product": (
  1141→                serializers.EcommProductSerializer(male_partner_voucher_product).data
  1142→                if male_partner_voucher_product
  1143→                else None
  1144→            ),
  1145→        }
  1146→        return Response(data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
  1147→
  1148→    @action(methods=["get"], detail=True, url_path="treatment-recommendations-lightweight")
  1149→    def treatment_recommendations_lightweight(self, request, hash=None):
  1150→        """
  1151→        Lightweight endpoint that returns only product slugs instead of full product data
  1152→        for improved performance. Returns the same structure as treatment-recommendations
  1153→        but with product slugs only.

  1155→        test = self.get_object()
  1156→        care_eligible = get_test_eligible_for_care(test)

  1158→
  1159→        if not care_eligible and not a_la_care_eligible:
  1160→            return Response({}, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
  1161→
  1162→        # Get or create the recommended treatment program once to avoid repeated expensive computations
  1163→        recommended_treatment_program = None
  1164→        if hasattr(test, "testresult") and care_eligible:
  1165→            # Use select_related to reduce database queries
  1166→            test.testresult.refresh_from_db()
  1167→            recommended_treatment_program = get_or_create_recommended_treatment_program(
  1168→                test.testresult
  1169→            )
  1170→            # Prefetch the treatments to avoid N+1 queries
  1171→            recommended_treatments = list(
  1172→                recommended_treatment_program.recommended_treatments.select_related(
  1173→                    "ecomm_product", "bundle_variant"

  1463→    @action(methods=["post"], detail=True, url_path="provider-release")

  1482→    @action(methods=["get"], detail=True, url_path="urine-results")
  1483→    def urine_results(self, request, hash=None):
  1484→        """

  1486→        Returns data for "Your Diagnosis" and "Your Detailed Results" sections.

  1488→        test = self.get_object()
  1489→
  1490→        # Verify this is a urine test
  1491→        if not test.is_urine_test:
  1492→            return Response(
  1493→                "This endpoint is only for urine tests", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST

  1495→
  1496→        # Make sure test is ready
  1497→        if test.status != UrineTest.STATUS_READY:
  1498→            return Response("Results not ready", status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND)
  1499→
  1500→        # If user is viewing the test results for the first time, record it

  1547→
  1548→        return Response(result_data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)

  1551→def _get_cst_health_associations(valencia_type):
  1552→    if not valencia_type:

  1554→
  1555→    # TODO: could cache this based on valencia type

  1562→        many=True,

1→import logging
     2→
     3→from django.contrib.auth.models import User
     4→from django.http import HttpResponse, HttpResponseRedirect
     5→from django.utils.deprecation import MiddlewareMixin
     6→
     7→from rest_framework_simplejwt.exceptions import InvalidToken
     8→from rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens import RefreshToken
     9→
    10→from api.v1.authentication import EvvyJWTAuthentication
    11→
    12→request_logger = logging.getLogger("evvy.request.auth")
    13→
    14→
    15→class HttpResponseUnauthorized(HttpResponse):
    16→    status_code = 401
    17→
    18→
    19→def get_jwt_user(request):
    20→    if request.user and request.user.is_authenticated:
    21→        return request.user
    22→    jwt_authentication = EvvyJWTAuthentication()
    23→    resp = jwt_authentication.authenticate(request)
    24→    if resp:
    25→        return resp[0]
    26→    return None
    27→
    28→
    29→def is_user_allowed_to_impersonate(user):
    30→    return user.is_staff and user.groups.filter(name="user-impersonation").exists()
    31→
    32→
    33→class ImpersonateMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin):
    34→    def process_request(self, request):
    35→        if "__impersonate" in request.GET:
    36→            try:
    37→                user = get_jwt_user(request)
    38→                if is_user_allowed_to_impersonate(user):
    39→                    try:
    40→                        impersonated_user = User.objects.get(username=request.GET["__impersonate"])
    41→                    except User.DoesNotExist:
    42→                        request_logger.warning(
    43→                            "Attempted impersonation for %s: User does not exist"
    44→                            % request.GET["__impersonate"]
    45→                        )
    46→                        return
    47→                    refresh = RefreshToken.for_user(impersonated_user)
    48→                    access_token = str(refresh.access_token)
    49→                    request.META["HTTP_AUTHORIZATION"] = f"Bearer {access_token}"
    50→            except InvalidToken as e:
    51→                return HttpResponseUnauthorized("invalid token")
    52→
    53→    def process_response(self, request, response):
    54→        if "__impersonate" in request.GET and is_user_allowed_to_impersonate(request.user):
    55→            if isinstance(response, HttpResponseRedirect):
    56→                location = response["Location"]
    57→                if "?" in location:
    58→                    location += "&"
    59→                else:
    60→                    location += "?"
    61→                location += f"__impersonate={request.GET['__impersonate']}"
    62→                response["Location"] = location
    63→        return response
    64→

    81→def evvy_logout_api_view(*args, **kwargs):
    82→    def decorator(view_func):
    83→        # Apply the authentication_classes decorator
    84→        view_func = authentication_classes([SessionAuthentication])(view_func)
    85→
    86→        # Apply the api_view decorator last
    87→        view_func = api_view(*args, **kwargs)(view_func)
    88→        return view_func
    89→
    90→    return decorator

   108→class ResultsManagerRequired(BasePermission):
   109→    message = "Must be an Evvy Results Manager to access this resource."

   111→    def has_permission(self, request, view):
   112→        return request.user.groups.filter(name=GROUP_NAME_RESULTS_MANAGERS).exists()

   115→def has_coach_user_assignment(coach, user_id) -> bool:
   116→    # If they have the results manager permission, they can access any user
   117→    if coach.groups.filter(name=GROUP_NAME_RESULTS_MANAGERS).exists():
   118→        return True
   119→
   120→    assignment = CoachUserAssignment.objects.filter(
   121→        coach=coach,
   122→        user_id=user_id,
   123→        deleted=False,
   124→    ).first()
   125→
   126→    if not assignment:
   127→        return False
   128→
   129→    return assignment.expiry_date is None or assignment.expiry_date >= timezone.now()
   130→

   132→class CoachUserAssignmentRequired(BasePermission):
   133→    message = "Must have a CoachUserAssignment or be a ResultsManager for this user to access this resource."
   134→
   135→    def has_permission(self, request, view):
   136→        coach = request.user
   137→        user_id = view.kwargs.get("pk")
   138→
   139→        # Only enforce for detail views
   140→        if not user_id:
   141→            return True
   142→
   143→        return has_coach_user_assignment(coach, user_id)

   163→class UserSummaryViewSet(EvvyInternalReadOnlyViewSet):
   164→    serializer_class = UserSummarySerializer
   165→    queryset = User.objects.order_by("-date_joined")
   166→    pagination_class = CustomPageNumberPagination
   167→    permission_classes = [IsAuthenticated, CoachUserAssignmentRequired]

   169→    @extend_schema(
   170→        parameters=[
   171→            OpenApiParameter(name="search", type=OpenApiTypes.STR, location=OpenApiParameter.QUERY),
   172→            OpenApiParameter(name="page", type=OpenApiTypes.INT, location=OpenApiParameter.QUERY),
   173→            OpenApiParameter(
   174→                name="page_size", type=OpenApiTypes.INT, location=OpenApiParameter.QUERY
   175→            ),
   176→        ],
   177→        responses=UserSummaryListSerializer(many=True),
   178→    )
   179→    def list(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
   180→        queryset = self.queryset
   181→        search_terms = request.query_params.get("search")
   182→
   183→        coach = request.user
   184→
   185→        # If the user is a results manager, they can see all users, otherwise only the users they are assigned to
   186→        if not coach.groups.filter(name=GROUP_NAME_RESULTS_MANAGERS).exists():
   187→            user_ids = CoachUserAssignment.objects.filter(
   188→                Q(expiry_date__isnull=True) | Q(expiry_date__gte=timezone.now()),
   189→                coach=coach,
   190→                deleted=False,
   191→            ).values_list("user_id", flat=True)
   192→
   193→            queryset = queryset.filter(id__in=user_ids)
   194→
   195→        if search_terms:
   196→            queryset = queryset.filter(
   197→                Q(email__icontains=search_terms)
   198→                | Q(first_name__icontains=search_terms)
   199→                | Q(last_name__icontains=search_terms)
   200→                | Q(test__hash__icontains=search_terms)
   201→                | Q(consult__uid__icontains=search_terms)
   202→            ).distinct()
   203→
   204→        # Get the paginated response
   205→        page = self.paginate_queryset(queryset)
   206→        if page is not None:
   207→            serializer = UserSummaryListSerializer(page, many=True)
   208→            return self.get_paginated_response(serializer.data)
   209→
   210→        serializer = UserSummaryListSerializer(queryset, many=True)
   211→        return Response(serializer.data)

   278→class AIMessageViewSet(EvvyInternalViewSet):
   279→    permission_classes = [IsAuthenticated, CoachUserAssignmentForAIRequired]
   280→
   281→    def get_serializer_class(self):
   282→        message_type = self.request.query_params.get("type")
   283→        if message_type == "assistant":
   284→            return AssistantThreadMessageSerializer
   285→        else:
   286→            return AISummarySerializer
   287→
   288→    def get_queryset(self):
   289→        message_type = self.request.query_params.get("type")
   290→
   291→        if message_type == "support":
   292→            return AISummary.objects.filter(
   293→                user__id=int(self.kwargs["user_id"], summary_type=AISummary.TYPE_SUPPORT)
   294→            )
   295→        elif message_type == "survey":
   296→            return AISummary.objects.filter(
   297→                user__id=int(self.kwargs["user_id"], summary_type=AISummary.TYPE_SURVEY)
   298→            )
   299→        elif message_type == "assistant":
   300→            return AssistantThreadMessage.objects.filter(
   301→                user__id=int(self.kwargs["user_id"])
   302→            ).order_by("-create_date")
   303→        else:
   304→            raise Http404
   305→
   306→    @extend_schema(
   307→        responses=PolymorphicProxySerializer(
   308→            serializers=[AISummarySerializer, AssistantThreadMessageSerializer],
   309→            resource_type_field_name=None,
   310→            component_name="AIMessage",
   311→        ),
   312→        request=PolymorphicProxySerializer(
   313→            serializers=[
   314→                inline_serializer(
   315→                    "CreateAssistantMessageSerializer",
   316→                    fields={
   317→                        "message": serializers.CharField(),
   318→                    },
   319→                ),
   320→                inline_serializer(
   321→                    "CreateSummarySerializer",
   322→                    fields={
   323→                        "reference_id": serializers.CharField(),
   324→                        "force_regenerate": serializers.BooleanField(required=False),
   325→                    },
   326→                ),
   327→            ],
   328→            resource_type_field_name=None,
   329→            component_name="CreateAIMessage",
   330→        ),
   331→    )
   332→    def create(self, request, user_id=None):
   333→        auth_user = request.user
   334→        user = User.objects.filter(id=user_id).first()
   335→        message_type = self.request.query_params.get("type")
   336→        if message_type == "assistant":
   337→            # Check permissions
   338→            if not auth_user.groups.filter(name=GROUP_NAME_USER_SUMMARY_ASSISTANT_USERS).exists():
   339→                return Response(
   340→                    status=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
   341→                    data={
   342→                        "error": "Must be an Evvy User Summary Assistant User to access this resource."
   343→                    },
   344→                )
   345→
   346→            assistant_thread = user.assistantthread_set.first()
   347→
   348→            if not assistant_thread:
   349→                return Response(
   350→                    status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
   351→                    data={"error": "Assistant thread not found."},
   352→                )
   353→
   354→            new_message = request.data.get("message")
   355→            if not new_message:
   356→                return Response(
   357→                    status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
   358→                    data={"error": "Message is required."},
   359→                )
   360→
   361→            new_message = ask_sally(assistant_thread, user, new_message)
   362→
   363→            return Response(AssistantThreadMessageSerializer(new_message).data)
   364→        elif message_type == "support" or message_type == "survey":
   365→            reference_id = request.data.get("reference_id")
   366→            force_regenerate = request.data.get("force_regenerate")
   367→            if message_type == "support":
   368→                if not reference_id:
   369→                    return Response(
   370→                        status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
   371→                        data={"error": "conversation_id is required."},
   372→                    )
   373→
   374→                summary_type = AISummary.TYPE_SUPPORT
   375→                summary_method = summarize_intercom_interactions_for_intercom_conversation_id
   376→
   377→            elif message_type == "survey":
   378→                if not reference_id:
   379→                    return Response(
   380→                        status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
   381→                        data={"error": "survey_id is required."},
   382→                    )
   383→
   384→                summary_type = AISummary.TYPE_SURVEY
   385→                summary_method = summarize_survicate_response_for_survey_id
   386→
   387→            if force_regenerate:
   388→                openai_response, prompt_version, model = summary_method(reference_id)
   389→            else:
   390→                existing_message = AISummary.objects.filter(
   391→                    reference_id=reference_id, summary_type=summary_type, user=user
   392→                ).first()
   393→
   394→                if existing_message:
   395→                    logger.info(f"Summary for {message_type} {reference_id} found in db.")
   396→                    return Response(AISummarySerializer(existing_message).data)
   397→
   398→                openai_response, prompt_version, model = summary_method(reference_id)
   399→
   400→            # Handle case where summary generation failed gracefully
   401→            if openai_response is None or prompt_version is None:
   402→                # Return 200 with empty data instead of error to keep timeline API working
   403→                return Response(status=status.HTTP_200_OK, data=None)
   404→
   405→            content = openai_response.choices[0].message.content
   406→            usage = openai_response.usage
   407→
   408→            cost = get_cost_from_openai_response(openai_response, model)
   409→            summary = AISummary.objects.create(
   410→                user=user,
   411→                content=content,
   412→                message_id=openai_response.id,
   413→                prompt_tokens=usage.prompt_tokens,
   414→                completion_tokens=usage.completion_tokens,
   415→                summary_type=summary_type,
   416→                cost=cost,
   417→                reference_id=reference_id,
   418→                prompt_version=prompt_version,
   419→                model=model,
   420→            )
   421→            logger.info(f"Summary for {message_type} {reference_id} created.")
   422→
   423→            return Response(AISummarySerializer(summary).data)
   424→
   425→        else:
   426→            return Response(
   427→                status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
   428→                data={"error": "Invalid message type."},
   429→            )
   430→

   529→class ResultsViewSet(ResultManagerViewSet):
   530→    serializer_class = TestResultSerializer
   531→    queryset = TestResult.objects.filter(
   532→        test__status=Test.STATUS_PROCESSING, test__healthcontext__isnull=False
   533→    ).order_by("-test__prioritize", "create_date")
   534→
   535→    lookup_field = "test__hash"
   536→
   537→    @extend_schema(responses=TestResultListSerializer(many=True))
   538→    def list(self, request):
   539→        queryset = self.get_queryset()
   540→        serializer = TestResultListSerializer(queryset, many=True)
   541→        return Response(serializer.data)
   542→
   543→    def retrieve(self, request, test__hash=None):
   544→        queryset = TestResult.objects.filter(
   545→            test__hash=test__hash, test__healthcontext__isnull=False
   546→        )
   547→
   548→        if queryset.count() == 0:
   549→            return Response(status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND)
   550→        elif queryset.count() > 1:
   551→            return Response(
   552→                status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
   553→                data={"error": "Multiple test results found for this test hash."},
   554→            )
   555→        test_result = queryset[0]
   556→        # Create a tagset for the result if it doesn't already have one (only needed for old results)
   557→        if not hasattr(test_result, "tag_set"):
   558→            create_test_result_tagset(test_result.test.hash)
   559→
   560→        serializer = TestResultSerializer(queryset[0])
   561→        return Response(serializer.data)
   562→
   563→    @extend_schema(request=PlanItemSerializer(many=True))
   564→    @action(methods=["put"], detail=True, lookup_field="test__hash")
   565→    def plan_items(self, request, test__hash=None):
   566→        """
   567→        Update the plan items for a test by using PlanItemExceptions and PlanItemAdditions
   568→        """
   569→
   570→        test_result = self.get_object()
   571→        test = test_result.test
   572→        plan_items_data = request.data
   573→
   574→        original_plan_items = plan_items_for_test(test_result.test)
   575→
   576→        # Find the differences between the original plan items and the new plan items
   577→        plan_item_ids_to_add = []
   578→        plan_items_ids_to_remove = []
   579→
   580→        for plan_item in plan_items_data:
   581→            if plan_item.get("id") not in [item.id for item in original_plan_items]:
   582→                plan_item_ids_to_add.append(plan_item.get("id"))
   583→
   584→        for plan_item in original_plan_items:
   585→            if plan_item.id not in [item.get("id") for item in plan_items_data]:
   586→                plan_items_ids_to_remove.append(plan_item.id)
   587→
   588→        # Add the new plan items
   589→        for plan_item_id in plan_item_ids_to_add:
   590→            # If an exception exists, delete it
   591→            PlanItemException.objects.filter(test=test, plan_item__id=plan_item_id).delete()
   592→
   593→            plan_item = PlanItem.objects.get(id=plan_item_id)
   594→            PlanItemAddition.objects.create(test=test, plan_item=plan_item)
   595→
   596→        # Remove the old plan items
   597→        for plan_item_id in plan_items_ids_to_remove:
   598→            # If an addition exists, delete it
   599→            PlanItemAddition.objects.filter(test=test, plan_item__id=plan_item_id).delete()
   600→
   601→            plan_item = PlanItem.objects.get(id=plan_item_id)
   602→            PlanItemException.objects.create(test=test, plan_item=plan_item)
   603→
   604→        return Response(status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
   605→
   606→    @extend_schema(
   607→        responses=inline_serializer(

   614→        request=None,

   616→    @action(methods=["post"], detail=True, lookup_field="test__hash")
   617→    def release(self, request, test__hash=None):
   618→        test_result = self.get_object()
   619→        release_results = release_test_results(test_result)
   620→        if release_results["status"] == "success":
   621→            return Response(
   622→                status=status.HTTP_200_OK,
   623→                data={
   624→                    "message": "Successfully released test results.",
   625→                    "status": "success",
   626→                },
   627→            )
   628→        elif release_results["status"] == "warning":
   629→            return Response(
   630→                status=status.HTTP_200_OK,
   631→                data={"message": release_results["message"], "status": "warning"},
   632→            )
   633→        else:
   634→            return Response(
   635→                status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
   636→                data={"error": "Unable to release test results."},
   637→            )

    20→class CartViewSet(EvvyUserViewSet):
    21→    """
    22→    API view for ecommerce shenanigans. Get latest active cart for user, add to cart, checkout etc.
    23→    """
    24→
    25→    serializer_class = serializers.CartSerializer
    26→
    27→    def get_queryset(self):
    28→        # only gets active carts, ordered by most recently active
    29→        return self.request.user.cart_set.filter(status=Cart.STATUS_ACTIVE).order_by(
    30→            "-last_active_at"
    31→        )
    32→
    33→    def create(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
    34→        # try to get the latest active cart, if none exists, then create a new one
    35→        cart = self.get_queryset().first()
    36→        cart_was_created = False
    37→        if not cart or cart.status != Cart.STATUS_ACTIVE:
    38→            cart = Cart.objects.create(user=request.user, status=Cart.STATUS_ACTIVE)
    39→            cart_was_created = True
    40→
    41→        if cart_was_created:
    42→            session_id = request.headers.get("X-Session-ID") or request.META.get(
    43→                "HTTP_X_SESSION_ID"
    44→            )
    45→            track_cart_creation(cart, source="api", session_id=session_id)
    46→
    47→        data = self.serializer_class(cart).data
    48→        return Response(data, status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)

    50→    def update(self, request, *args, **kwargs):

    60→        data = request.data

    14→class ConsentView(EvvyUserAPIView):

    16→    Allows user to accept/deny a consent
    17→    We started officially tracking consent on 2022-11-08
    18→    """
    19→
    20→    def post(self, request, format=None):
    21→        user = request.user
    22→
    23→        agreed = request.data.get("agreed", None)
    24→        if agreed is None or not isinstance(agreed, bool):
    25→            return Response("Invalid consent agreed response", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
    26→
    27→        # if user has already submitted their response, don't let them change it and just return successful
    28→        if user_has_answered_research_consent(user):
    29→            return Response("success", status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
    30→
    31→        # otherwise, create the object and store the response
    32→        consent = Consent.objects.create(
    33→            user=user,
    34→            consent_type=Consent.CONSENT_TYPE_RESEARCH,
    35→            agreed=agreed,
    36→            consent_submitted_at=get_current_time(),
    37→        )
    38→        return Response("success", status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)

1→from django.conf import settings

     6→
     7→from accounts.models import OrderTransferVerification
     8→from api.v1.serializers.order import OrderSerializer
     9→from api.v1.views.views import EvvyUserAPIView
    10→from ecomm.models.order import Order
    11→from ecomm.services.cart import remove_order_from_current_associated_consults
    12→from ecomm.tasks import add_order_treatments_to_latest_existing_consult_if_exists_or_create_consult
    13→from transactional_email.constants import TEMPLATE_ACCOUNT_ORDER_TRANSFER
    14→from transactional_email.utils import get_email_context, send_templated_email
    15→
    16→
    17→class AccountOrderTransferVerification(EvvyUserAPIView):
    18→    """
    19→    For initiating order transfers between orders with an email that does not match their current evvy account email.
    20→    Currently, OrderTransferVerification emails are sent only for ungated RX customers.
    21→    """
    22→
    23→    def get(self, request, format=None):
    24→        """
    25→        Get open orders that have had transferred initiated by the user.
    26→        - Email is not yet verified by user
    27→        - Order is not yet attached to any user
    28→        - Order has not been cancelled
    29→        """
    30→        user = request.user
    31→        unverified_emails = OrderTransferVerification.objects.filter(user=user, verified=False)
    32→
    33→        orders = []
    34→        for transfer_email in unverified_emails:
    35→            order = transfer_email.ecomm_order
    36→            if order.status != Order.STATUS_CANCELLED and order not in orders:
    37→                has_verified_email = OrderTransferVerification.objects.filter(
    38→                    user=user, order_email=order.email, verified=True, ecomm_order=order
    39→                ).exists()
    40→                if not has_verified_email and order.order_type == Order.ORDER_TYPE_UNGATED_RX:
    41→                    orders.append(order)
    42→
    43→        serializer = OrderSerializer(orders, many=True)
    44→        return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
    45→
    46→    def post(self, request, format=None):
    47→        """
    48→        Send verification emails to the order email for each order passed in.
    49→        """
    50→        user = request.user
    51→        order_numbers = request.data.get("orders", [])
    52→        orders = Order.objects.filter(order_number__in=order_numbers)
    53→
    54→        for order in orders:
    55→            # create instead of get_or_create in case they click on a previous order email
    56→            account_transfer_verification_email = OrderTransferVerification.objects.create(
    57→                user=user,
    58→                ecomm_order=order,
    59→                order_email=order.email,
    60→                verified=False,
    61→            )
    62→
    63→            # send an e-mail to the user's order email for each order they need to verify
    64→            verify_url = "{}/order-transfer-confirm/?hash={}".format(
    65→                settings.SITE_URL, account_transfer_verification_email.hash
    66→            )
    67→
    68→            context = get_email_context(
    69→                user,
    70→                {
    71→                    "order_number": order.order_number,
    72→                    "order_transfer_email": user.email,
    73→                    "verify_url": verify_url,
    74→                },
    75→            )
    76→            success, error = send_templated_email(
    77→                order.email, TEMPLATE_ACCOUNT_ORDER_TRANSFER, context=context
    78→            )
    79→
    80→            if error:
    81→                return Response(error, status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
    82→
    83→        return Response("Order transfer emails sent", status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
    84→
    85→
    86→class AccountOrderTransferConfirmVerification(APIView):
    87→    """
    88→    Allows the user to confirm an order transfer to their account.
    89→    User can be unauthenticated.
    90→    """
    91→
    92→    def post(self, request, format=None):
    93→        verification_hash = request.data.get("hash", None)
    94→        if not verification_hash:
    95→            return Response("Missing hash", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
    96→
    97→        # Get the verification email
    98→        verification_email = OrderTransferVerification.objects.filter(
    99→            hash=verification_hash
   100→        ).first()
   101→
   102→        if not verification_email:
   103→            return Response("Invalid hash", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
   104→
   105→        # Also check if they have any other outstanding orders that need to be verified
   106→        # Order should have the same Evvy user and same order email to be transferred.
   107→        evvy_account_user = verification_email.user
   108→        order_email = verification_email.order_email
   109→
   110→        all_orders_to_attach = OrderTransferVerification.objects.filter(
   111→            user=evvy_account_user, order_email=order_email, verified=False
   112→        )
   113→
   114→        # User could have multiple order verification emails for each order; verify/attach all of them
   115→        attached_orders = []
   116→        for order_to_verify in all_orders_to_attach:
   117→            # First attach the user to the ecomm order if it doesn't have a user already
   118→            order = order_to_verify.ecomm_order
   119→
   120→            # If there is an existing user on the order different than the account it's being attached to, remove their order from any consults
   121→            if order.user and order.user != evvy_account_user:
   122→                remove_order_from_current_associated_consults(order)
   123→
   124→            # Attach the user to the order and combine/create their consult
   125→            if order not in attached_orders:
   126→                order.user = evvy_account_user
   127→                order.save()
   128→                add_order_treatments_to_latest_existing_consult_if_exists_or_create_consult.delay(
   129→                    evvy_account_user.id, order.order_number
   130→                )
   131→                attached_orders.append(order)
   132→
   133→            # Mark order verification object as verified
   134→            order_to_verify.verified = True
   135→            order_to_verify.save()
   136→
   137→        # If no orders were newly attached:
   138→        # return just the single order associated with this hash so we can display it on the frontend
   139→        if len(attached_orders) == 0:
   140→            attached_orders.append(verification_email.ecomm_order)
   141→
   142→        serializer = OrderSerializer(attached_orders, many=True)
   143→        return Response(
   144→            {
   145→                "orders": serializer.data,
   146→                "evvy_email": evvy_account_user.email,
   147→                "order_email": order_email,
   148→            },
   149→            status=status.HTTP_200_OK,
   150→        )
   151→

   528→def _verify_viome_webhook(access_token: str, secret_key: str) -> bool:
   529→    """
   530→    Verify Viome webhook using static key verification.
   531→
   532→    Note: Viome is using Shopify Flow which doesn't have the capability of generating
   533→    HMAC signatures, so we use static key verification instead.
   534→    """
   535→    if not access_token or not secret_key:
   536→        return False
   537→
   538→    return hmac.compare_digest(access_token, secret_key)

   563→def _verify_typeform_webhook(data: bytes, signature_header: str, secret_key: str) -> bool:
   564→    """
   565→    Verify Typeform webhook signature.
   566→
   567→    Typeform sends the signature in the format: sha256={base64_encoded_hash}
   568→    where the hash is HMAC-SHA256 of the raw request body.
   569→
   570→    Args:
   571→        data: Raw request body as bytes
   572→        signature_header: The Typeform-Signature header value (e.g., "sha256=...")
   573→        secret_key: The webhook secret key configured in Typeform
   574→
   575→    Returns:
   576→        True if signature is valid, False otherwise
   577→    """
   578→    if not signature_header or not secret_key:
   579→        return False
   580→
   581→    digest = hmac.new(secret_key.encode("utf-8"), data, digestmod=hashlib.sha256).digest()
   582→    computed_signature = base64.b64encode(digest).decode("utf-8")
   583→
   584→    # Typeform sends signature in format: sha256={base64_hash}
   585→    expected_signature = f"sha256={computed_signature}"
   586→
   587→    return hmac.compare_digest(expected_signature, signature_header)

    34→        except Exception as e:

    36→            return Response(
    37→                {"error": "Failed to fetch product"}, 
    38→                status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
    39→            )

    13→class PDPConfigurationViewSet(ReadOnlyModelViewSet):
    14→    """
    15→    ViewSet for PDP configurations
    16→    """
    17→
    18→    queryset = PDPConfiguration.objects.filter(is_active=True)
    19→    serializer_class = PDPConfigurationSerializer
    20→
    21→    def get_serializer_class(self):
    22→        if self.action == "list":
    23→            return PDPConfigurationListSerializer
    24→        return PDPConfigurationSerializer
    25→
    26→    def get_queryset(self):
    27→        queryset = super().get_queryset()
    28→        # Filter by slug if provided
    29→        slug = self.request.query_params.get("slug", None)
    30→        if slug:
    31→            queryset = queryset.filter(slug=slug)
    32→
    33→        # Filter by care product if provided
    34→        care_product_slug = self.request.query_params.get("care_product_slug", None)
    35→        if care_product_slug:
    36→            queryset = queryset.filter(care_product__slug=care_product_slug)
    37→
    38→        # Filter by ecomm product if provided
    39→        ecomm_product_sku = self.request.query_params.get("ecomm_product_sku", None)
    40→        if ecomm_product_sku:
    41→            queryset = queryset.filter(ecomm_product__sku=ecomm_product_sku)
    42→
    43→        return queryset.order_by("-is_featured", "name")
    44→
    45→    @action(detail=False, methods=["get"], url_path="by-care-product/(?P<care_product_slug>[^/.]+)")
    46→    def by_care_product(self, request, care_product_slug=None):
    47→        """
    48→        Get PDP configuration by care product slug
    49→        """
    50→        try:
    51→            pdp_config = self.get_queryset().filter(care_product__slug=care_product_slug).first()
    52→            if pdp_config:
    53→                serializer = PDPConfigurationSerializer(pdp_config)
    54→                return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
    55→            else:
    56→                return Response(
    57→                    {"error": "No PDP configuration found for this care product"},
    58→                    status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
    59→                )
    60→        except Exception:
    61→            return Response(
    62→                {"error": "Error fetching PDP configuration"},
    63→                status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
    64→            )
    65→
    66→    @action(
    67→        detail=False, methods=["get"], url_path="by-ecomm-product/(?P<ecomm_product_sku>[^/.]+)"
    68→    )
    69→    def by_ecomm_product(self, request, ecomm_product_sku=None):
    70→        """
    71→        Get PDP configuration by ecomm product SKU
    72→        """
    73→        try:
    74→            pdp_config = self.get_queryset().filter(ecomm_product__sku=ecomm_product_sku).first()
    75→            if pdp_config:
    76→                serializer = PDPConfigurationSerializer(pdp_config)
    77→                return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
    78→            else:
    79→                return Response(
    80→                    {"error": "No PDP configuration found for this ecomm product"},
    81→                    status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
    82→                )
    83→        except Exception:
    84→            return Response(
    85→                {"error": "Error fetching PDP configuration"},
    86→                status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
    87→            )
    88→
    89→    @action(detail=False, methods=["get"], url_path="by-slug/(?P<slug>[^/.]+)")
    90→    def by_slug(self, request, slug=None):
    91→        """
    92→        Get PDP configuration by slug
    93→        """
    94→        try:
    95→            pdp_config = self.get_queryset().get(slug=slug)
    96→            if pdp_config:
    97→                serializer = self.get_serializer(pdp_config)
    98→                return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
    99→            else:
   100→                return Response(
   101→                    {"error": "No PDP configuration found for this slug"},
   102→                    status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
   103→                )
   104→        except PDPConfiguration.DoesNotExist:
   105→            return Response(
   106→                {"error": "Error fetching PDP configurations"},
   107→                status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
   108→            )
   109→
   110→    @action(detail=False, methods=["get"], url_path="featured")
   111→    def featured(self, request):
   112→        """
   113→        Get featured PDP configurations
   114→        """
   115→        featured_configs = self.get_queryset().filter(is_featured=True)
   116→        serializer = self.get_serializer(featured_configs, many=True)
   117→        return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
   118→

    82→    def create(self, request):

    98→            ),
    99→            add_expanded_pcr=add_expanded_pcr,
   100→        )
   101→
   102→        data = self.serializer_class(provider_test_order, context={"request": request}).data

   104→        send_provider_ordered_single_test_notifications.delay(

   147→        return Response(data, status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)

   149→    @action(detail=False, methods=["post"])
   150→    def bulk_create(self, request):
   151→        provider = request.user
   152→        num_ordered = request.data.get("num_ordered")
   153→        add_sti = request.data.get("add_sti", False)
   154→
   155→        try:
   156→            num_ordered = int(num_ordered) if num_ordered is not None else 0
   157→            if num_ordered <= 0:
   158→                return Response(
   159→                    "Number ordered must be greater than 0", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST
   160→                )
   161→            if num_ordered > MAX_BULK_ORDER_SIZE:
   162→                return Response(
   163→                    f"Number ordered cannot exceed {MAX_BULK_ORDER_SIZE:,} tests per bulk order",
   164→                    status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
   165→                )
   166→        except (TypeError, ValueError):
   167→            return Response(
   168→                "Number ordered must be a valid integer", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST
   169→            )
   170→
   171→        # Determine provider location and select appropriate product variant gid
   172→        provider_profile = get_provider_profile(provider)
   173→
   174→        is_canadian = provider_profile.is_canadian
   175→        if is_canadian:
   176→            merchandise_variant_gid = SHOPIFY_PROVIDER_BILLED_CANADA_VHT_VARIANT_ID
   177→        else:
   178→            merchandise_variant_gid = (
   179→                SHOPIFY_PROVIDER_BILLED_EXPAN_VARIANT_ID
   180→                if add_sti
   181→                else SHOPIFY_PROVIDER_BILLED_VHT_VARIANT_ID
   182→            )
   183→
   184→        bulk_order_id = uuid.uuid4()
   185→
   186→        try:
   187→            from ecomm.shopify.shopify import ShopifyAPIClient
   188→
   189→            line_items = [
   190→                {
   191→                    "quantity": num_ordered,
   192→                    "merchandiseId": merchandise_variant_gid,
   193→                }
   194→            ]
   195→
   196→            custom_attributes = {
   197→                "bulk_order_id": str(bulk_order_id),
   198→                "order_type": "provider_bulk",
   199→                "checkout_source": Order.CHECKOUT_SOURCE_PROVIDER,
   200→            }
   201→
   202→            def _get_bulk_tier(n: int) -> str:
   203→                for tier, threshold in sorted(BULK_TIER_THRESHOLDS.items(), key=lambda kv: kv[1]):
   204→                    if n <= threshold:
   205→                        return tier
   206→                return "TIER_4"  # Fallback, though this shouldn't happen
   207→
   208→            tier_key = _get_bulk_tier(num_ordered)
   209→            if is_canadian:
   210→                product_key = "CANADA"
   211→            else:
   212→                product_key = "EXPAN" if add_sti else "VHT"
   213→
   214→            discount_code = PROVIDER_BULK_DISCOUNT_CODES.get(product_key, {}).get(tier_key)
   215→            if not discount_code:
   216→                logger.warning(
   217→                    "No bulk discount code configured",
   218→                    extra={
   219→                        "product_key": product_key,
   220→                        "tier": tier_key,
   221→                        "provider_id": provider.id,
   222→                    },
   223→                )
   224→
   225→            shopify_client = ShopifyAPIClient()
   226→            checkout_url = shopify_client.get_checkout_url(
   227→                user=provider,
   228→                line_items=line_items,
   229→                custom_attributes=custom_attributes,
   230→                discount_code=discount_code,
   231→                buyer_country_code="CA" if is_canadian else "US",
   232→            )
   233→
   234→        except Exception:
   235→            logger.error(
   236→                f"Error generating provider bulk checkout URL for provider {provider.id}",
   237→                exc_info=True,
   238→            )
   239→            return Response(
   240→                "Failed to generate checkout URL.",
   241→                status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
   242→            )
   243→
   244→        def _notify_after_commit(provider_email, provider_id, num_ordered_val, order_ids):
   245→            send_provider_bulk_ordered_tests_notification.delay(

   262→            send_provider_ordered_test_klaviyo_event.delay(

   290→        except Exception:
   291→            logger.error(
   292→                f"Error creating ProviderTestOrder records for provider {provider.id} bulk order {bulk_order_id}",
   293→                exc_info=True,
   294→            )
   295→            return Response(
   296→                "Failed to create bulk order records.",
   297→                status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
   298→            )
   299→
   300→        return Response({"checkout_url": checkout_url}, status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
   301→
   302→    @action(detail=False, methods=["post"])
   303→    def remind_patient(self, request):
   304→        provider = request.user
   305→        patient_email = request.data.get("patient_email")
   306→
   307→        if not patient_email:
   308→            return Response("Missing patient email field", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
   309→
   310→        send_provider_reminded_patient_klaviyo_event.delay(
   311→            provider_id=provider.id, patient_email=patient_email
   312→        )
   313→        provider_profile = get_provider_profile(provider)
   314→        provider_magic_link_slug = provider_profile.magic_link_slug
   315→        track_event(
   316→            alias_name=patient_email,
   317→            event_name=BrazeEventName.PROVIDER_REMINDED_PATIENT,
   318→            properties={
   319→                "providerEmail": provider.email,
   320→                "providerMagicLinkSlug": provider_magic_link_slug,
   321→            },
   322→        )
   323→
   324→        return Response(status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
   325→
   326→    @action(detail=True, methods=["get"], url_path="plan-items")
   327→    def plan_items(self, _, pk=None):
   328→        provider_test_order = self.get_object()
   329→        test = provider_test_order.test
   330→
   331→        plan_items = plan_items_for_test(test)
   332→        plan_items_data = serializers.PlanItemSerializer(plan_items, many=True).data
   333→        return Response(plan_items_data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
   334→
   335→    @action(detail=True, methods=["post"], url_path="checkout-link")
   336→    def checkout_link(self, request, pk=None):
   337→        """
   338→        Return a provider-paid checkout URL for the given ProviderTestOrder.
   339→        """
   340→        provider_test_order = self.get_object()
   341→        if provider_test_order.order_method != ProviderTestOrder.PROVIDER_PAID:
   342→            return Response(
   343→                "Only provider-paid orders have a checkout link", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST
   344→            )
   345→
   346→        patient_email = getattr(provider_test_order, "patient_email", None)
   347→        if not patient_email:
   348→            return Response("Missing patient email", status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
   349→
   350→        try:
   351→            from providers.utils import get_provider_order_checkout_link
   352→
   353→            checkout_link = get_provider_order_checkout_link(
   354→                provider=provider_test_order.provider,
   355→                patient_email=patient_email,
   356→                add_expanded_pcr=provider_test_order.add_expanded_pcr,
   357→                provider_test_order_id=provider_test_order.id,
   358→                provider_paid=True,
   359→            )
   360→        except Exception:
   361→            return Response(
   362→                "Failed to generate checkout URL.", status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
   363→            )
   364→
   365→        return Response({"checkout_url": checkout_link}, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
   366→
   367→    @action(detail=True, methods=["post"], url_path="mark-downloaded")
   368→    def mark_downloaded(self, request, pk=None):
   369→        """
   370→        Mark a provider test order as downloaded by setting the downloaded_at timestamp.
   371→        Always updates to current time so downloading new results clears the highlight.
   372→        """
   373→        from django.utils import timezone
   374→
   375→        provider_test_order = self.get_object()
   376→        was_none = provider_test_order.downloaded_at is None
   377→
   378→        provider_test_order.downloaded_at = timezone.now()
   379→        provider_test_order.save(update_fields=["downloaded_at"])
   380→
   381→        if was_none:
   382→            return Response(status=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
   383→        return Response(status=status.HTTP_200_OK)
   384→
